locally_available_herbs.name: Bitter Leaf
locally_available_herbs.preparation: Juice fresh leaves, or boil as vegetable

locally_available_herbs.name: Moringa
locally_available_herbs.preparation: Leaf powder in smoothies, or cooked as vegetable

locally_available_herbs.name: Turmeric
locally_available_herbs.preparation: Fresh root in stews, or powder as tea

locally_available_herbs.name: Ginger
locally_available_herbs.preparation: Fresh in teas, grated in meals

locally_available_herbs.name: African Basil
locally_available_herbs.preparation: Fresh leaves in soups and stews

where_to_source: Local markets (Balogun market, Mile 12), herbal pharmacies (agbo sellers), health shops in major cities
typical_cost_band: NGN - Nigerian Naira. Most herbs and foods are very affordable at local markets.
dietary_norms: Starch-heavy traditional diet. Increasing awareness of protein and vegetable balance. Religious considerations (halal in North, Christian in South).
language_for_local_names: Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa (include multiple where possible)

breakfast: Egg and plantain scramble with vegetables
lunch: Brown rice with vegetable stew (efo riro) and grilled fish
Amala with ewedu and gbegiri (bean soup)
Jollof rice (brown rice version) with chicken and salad
dinner: Pepper soup (fish or goat) with yam
Grilled fish with roasted plantain and vegetables
Bean porridge (ewa agoyin) with fried plantain
